meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Vocabulary Set 1
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for 'The Cow and the Tiger' — all 16 words from Weeks 6–7 (named, beautiful, graze, shadowy, forest, angry, caught, sad, promise, attack, agreed, grassy, surprised, happy, protect, truth).

questions.question: What does 'beautiful' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' means very pretty. In the story, the cow named Chumpa was beautiful — she was a lovely, pretty cow.

questions.question: What does 'graze' mean?
options: To eat grass in a field
To fall asleep under a tree
To drink water from a river
questions.explanation: 'Graze' means to eat grass slowly in a field. Cows graze — they walk slowly in a field and eat grass.

questions.question: 'Shadowy' means a place with lots of bright light.
questions.explanation: 'Shadowy' means dark with little light. A shadowy forest has many trees that block the sunlight — it is dark and a little scary.

questions.question: The cave is _______, so I turn on my flashlight to see.
questions.explanation: 'Shadowy' means dark with little light. A shadowy place needs a light because it is too dark to see well.

questions.question: 'Named' means the same as:
options: Gave a special name to something
questions.explanation: 'Named' means gave a special name. The cow was 'named Chumpa' — someone gave her the name Chumpa.

questions.question: What does 'promise' mean?
options: To say you will definitely do something
questions.explanation: 'Promise' means to say you will definitely do something. The tiger promised not to attack the cow — he gave his word.

questions.question: A helmet can _______ your head when you ride a bike.
questions.hint: To keep someone or something safe
questions.explanation: 'Protect' means to keep safe. The farmer wanted to protect Chumpa from the tiger — to keep her safe from attack.

questions.question: 'Agreed' means someone said 'yes' and accepted a plan.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Agreed' means said yes to something. The tiger 'agreed' to the farmer's plan — he said yes to the deal.

questions.question: The tiger was _______ when the farmer tricked him. He was very mad!
questions.explanation: 'Angry' means feeling very mad. The tiger was angry because he felt he had been tricked by the farmer.

questions.question: It is important to always tell the _______. Don't lie!
questions.hint: Something that is real and not a lie
questions.explanation: 'Truth' means something that is real and correct — not a lie. 'Tell the truth' means to say what really happened.

questions.question: Sort these words — do they describe a FEELING or an ACTION?
questions.explanation: 'Angry,' 'surprised,' and 'sad' describe how a person feels (adjectives). 'Attack,' 'protect,' and 'agreed' are things a person does (verbs).

questions.question: The boy dropped the ball, but his friend _______ it before it hit the ground.
questions.explanation: 'Caught' means grabbed or held onto something. The friend caught the ball — grabbed it before it could fall.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The tiger made a promise not to attack.' A promise means you say you will definitely do (or not do) something.

questions.question: 'Grassy' and 'shadowy' are both adjectives because they describe what a place looks like.
questions.explanation: Yes! Both are adjectives. 'Grassy' describes a field covered with grass. 'Shadowy' describes a dark place with little light. Both words describe the appearance of a place.

questions.question: Sort these vocabulary words — are they ADJECTIVES (describing words) or NOUNS (things)?
questions.explanation: 'Grassy,' 'beautiful,' and 'shadowy' describe what things look like (adjectives). 'Truth,' 'forest,' and 'promise' are things you can name (nouns).
